repairSteps.title: Restore the route correction
repairSteps.statement: Drouet's report says that he was informed near Clermont that the carriage had turned toward Varennes rather than continuing toward Verdun. The report does not identify who supplied that correction.

repairSteps.title: Redirect the pursuit
repairSteps.statement: In Drouet's report, the route information enabled him and Guillaume to travel by side roads toward Varennes.

repairSteps.title: Deliver the warning
repairSteps.statement: Drouet reported warning an innkeeper in Varennes, who then alerted other people.

repairSteps.title: Reconstruct collective action
repairSteps.statement: The reviewed accounts describe officials, residents, and National Guardsmen taking part in the response after the alarm.

repairSteps.title: Reconstruct parallel local actions
repairSteps.statement: The reviewed records place blocked onward passage and passport inspection within one collective local response. The repair requires both actions as an authored reconstruction; the sources do not prove that either action alone, or the pair as a strict but-for cause, produced detention.

repairSteps.title: Restore collective detention
repairSteps.statement: In this bounded reconstruction, guarded detention follows the combined local response, including passage control, passport inspection, municipal action, gathered residents, and guards. The surviving sources do not prove either edge as a strict but-for cause. Neither incoming edge represents a sole physical arrest mechanism.

politicalMeaning.statement: Contemporary official, diplomatic, and radical records offered competing framings of the royal departure, including a kidnapping explanation and published challenges to it.
The September 1791 Constitution retained monarchical government, so the flight did not immediately end monarchy or determine every later outcome.

counterfactualBoundary.label: UNKNOWN
counterfactualBoundary.statement: The fictional branch ends after the carriage passes Varennes because the reviewed record does not establish a precise alternate future.

established.statement: The completed caseboard identifies the route-information handoff, while reviewed records support a reconstruction of collective action ending in guarded detention.
established.limitations: This is an authored synthesis of cited records, not a verbatim historical statement. The records are not fully independent corroboration.

established.statement: The reviewed sequence included local mobilization, blocked onward passage, passport inspection, and detention after the warning.
established.limitations: The records support this observed sequence; historical necessity belongs only to the game's explicitly fictional fracture model.

claimLimits.statement: The sources do not settle every part of Louis's private motive.
The reviewed record does not make later revolutionary outcomes inevitable.

debrief.finalStateBoundary: This summary reports final validated caseboard actions and the student's recorded submitted argument. It does not claim that the prose was historically adjudicated or reveal earlier beliefs or unrecorded reasoning.
debrief.teacherReviewBoundary: This deterministic learning summary is formative. Future AI-assisted comments require teacher review before grading use.
